message:
[HIGH] fcp-host: per-zone policy lookup so admin deny rules actually fire (br-flywheel_connectors-d4cij)

verify_live_request previously called simulate_policy_decision with
host_runtime_policy(request.zone_id.clone()) at bin/fcp-host.rs:1449
— a freshly-constructed ZonePolicyObject with every pattern list empty
and every transport allowed. Under that blank policy,
check_pattern_lists short-circuits on every is_empty() guard so the
only denial paths that remain are RevocationStaleFrontier /
CheckpointStaleFrontier freshness flags, taint-flag checks on risky+
safety tiers, and approval-token mismatches. principal_deny,
capability_deny, connector_deny, and capability_ceiling rules
configured anywhere outside the gateway literally cannot fire.

Worse: there was no zone-policy store in AppState at all, so even an
operator who tried to populate those fields had nowhere to put them.
The gate looked like enforcement but was structurally a no-op for
rule-based denials.

Fix:

1. AppState gains
     zone_policies: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ZoneId, ZonePolicyObject>>>
   plus an inherent
     async fn lookup_zone_policy(&self, zone_id: &ZoneId) -> ZonePolicyObject
   that returns the configured policy when registered, otherwise falls
   back to host_runtime_policy and emits
     warn!("zone_policy_fallback_to_permissive", ...)
   so operators can see in logs that rule-based denial is not active
   for that zone.

2. verify_live_request swaps the inline host_runtime_policy(...) for
   state.lookup_zone_policy(&request.zone_id).await before building
   the PolicySimulationInput.

3. All six exhaustive AppState construction sites (one in main, five
   in tests) initialize zone_policies to an empty map; the four
   sites that use struct-update syntax (..(*base_state).clone())
   inherit it automatically.

4. New regression test
   verify_live_request_applies_configured_zone_principal_deny:
   registers a zone policy with principal_deny = ["user:test"] for
   z:work, sends a structurally-valid request with subject "user:test",
   asserts the rejection message contains "policy denied" — proving
   the principal_deny pattern actually fires now.

Out of scope this commit (intentional, called out in the bead's fix
shape):
  - Admin endpoints to GET/PUT zone policies. The lookup hook is in
    place; operators can register policies via a future
    /admin/zones/:zone_id/policy or via process startup. Without those
    endpoints the table stays empty and the warn! fallback fires for
    every request — same behavior as today, but observable.
  - Persistence integration with admin_state's connector inventory.
    Intentionally separate so a connector-config rollout cannot
    accidentally drop policy state.

Build-status note: workspace `cargo check` is still blocked on the
unrelated fcp-async-core ReceiverState<T> Debug-bound issue noted in
earlier session commits. This commit's surface is mechanical: one
struct field + one inherent method + one call-site swap + bulk-insert
into six exhaustive constructors + one regression test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
